own_review.issue: These 'my requests' / request-detail endpoints (javadoc: 'isMyRequest — Only return requests created by the user calling the API') previously had NO permission gate in the service layer; the PR now requires APPROVE_TOPICS/APPROVE_TOPICS_CREATE/APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS (resp. APPROVE_CONNECTORS). Default user role happens to hold APPROVE_TOPICS/APPROVE_CONNECTORS, but any custom requestor-only role (REQUEST_* permissions only) loses the ability to view its own requests. Approver permissions are semantically the wrong gate for a requestor-facing endpoint; a REQUEST_* or VIEW_* permission should be in the allowed set.

@Order(Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) on the @interface has no effect on execution order (it would need to be on the @Aspect bean). Spring MVC argument resolution — including the expensive DB-hitting @Valid TopicRequestValidatorImpl — runs BEFORE the AOP advice, and the validator's own permission short-circuit was removed. Unauthorized callers of /createTopics now exercise full request validation (tenant lookups, topic-existence checks) and can receive detailed 400 validation messages (e.g. 'topic already exists') before/instead of the 401; the PR's own IT test had to configure tenant config so validation passes just to reach the permission check, confirming the ordering.

Coverage gap: these TopicController endpoints get no @PermissionAllowed and createClaimTopicRequest has no service-layer check either, while the connector counterpart /createClaimConnectorRequest IS annotated (REQUEST_CREATE_CONNECTORS). Inconsistent with the PR's stated goal of uniform permission checks on every API.

Annotation grants access with ANY of {APPROVE_*, APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS} (isNotAuthorizedUser(Set) is any-of via retainAll), but the retained service-layer checks still strictly require APPROVE_TOPICS / APPROVE_CONNECTORS. A user holding only APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS passes the aspect, then gets HTTP 200 + NOT_AUTHORIZED body from the service — two inconsistent authorization paths.

Extends jakarta.validation.ConstraintDeclarationException, which the Bean Validation spec reserves for constraint-declaration errors, not runtime authorization failures; semantically wrong parent (plain RuntimeException would do).

Behavioral/API contract change: deleteTopicRequests and deleteConnectorRequests previously returned HTTP 200 with NOT_AUTHORIZED body; they now return HTTP 401. Also 401 UNAUTHORIZED is used for an authorization (not authentication) failure — 403 FORBIDDEN is the correct status, though this matches the existing KlawNotAuthorizedException handler convention. New handler also omits the log.error every other handler in the class has.
throws KlawNotAuthorizedException declarations are now stale — checkIsAuthorized (the only thrower in this service) was deleted.

The 'if (null != permissionAllowed)' guard is dead code — the @annotation binding guarantees non-null. Also @Target includes ElementType.TYPE but the pointcut only matches method-level annotations, so a class-level @PermissionAllowed would silently not be enforced; groups()/payload() attributes are unused Bean-Validation cargo-culting.
Trivia: method name typo 'NotAuthoirzed' (mirrors existing typo at line 30); wildcard imports introduced in IT test; BuildProperties autowired in TopicAclControllerIT but never used.

points.point: Missing AspectJ/spring-boot-starter-aop dependency — aspect will never execute (critical)
points.note: False. spring-boot-starter-data-jpa (Boot 3.2.0 parent) transitively brings spring-boot-starter-aop/aspectjweaver, so Boot's AopAutoConfiguration weaves the aspect. The PR's own IT tests asserting 401 (which CI runs) prove the advice fires. Absence-fabrication.

points.point: PermissionConstraintException extends wrong base class ConstraintDeclarationException
approve/declineConnectorRequests: annotation OR-logic vs service-layer APPROVE_CONNECTORS-only check blocks APPROVE_ALL_REQUESTS_TEAMS holders
points.note: Verified at service lines 641/791; the 'confusing 401' detail is slightly off (service returns 200+NOT_AUTHORIZED body) but the core mismatch is real.

points.point: Redundant service-layer permission checks remain (lines 112, 508, 641, 791, 839), correctly excepting the conditional filter at 532
Stale throws KlawNotAuthorizedException declarations after checkIsAuthorized removal
points.note: Verified: only thrower in TopicControllerService deleted.

points.point: Removed validator tests may need replacement — ensure new ITs cover negative cases
points.note: Hedged; the new ITs do cover them.

missed: Approver-only permissions gate the requestor-facing /getTopicRequests, /topic/request/{id}, /getConnectorRequests endpoints — breaks requestor-only roles (own finding, most important issue in PR)
@Order on the @interface is ineffective and @Valid TopicRequestValidatorImpl now runs full validation before the permission aspect (validator's own auth short-circuit was removed)
/createClaimTopicRequest and /getTopicTeam left without any permission check while the connector claim endpoint is annotated
Human point: redundant null-check / signature cleanup in PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor
exceeds: Annotation vs service-layer permission mismatch for connector approve/decline
Precise inventory of residual service-layer checks incl. the non-blocking one to keep

points.point: @Order(HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE) on the @interface has no effect; must be on the @Aspect class
points.note: Verified — Spring derives advice order from the aspect bean, not from a custom annotation declaration.

points.point: New handler omits log.error unlike every other handler in KlawExceptionHandler
points.note: Verified — all five existing handlers log; auth failures via new path are silent.

points.point: groups()/payload() attributes copied from Bean Validation are never read — dead noise
Residual inline isNotAuthorizedUser checks in KafkaConnectControllerService — migration incomplete, dual authorization paths
points.note: Substance verified (createConnectorRequest at 112, restartConnector 508, approve 641, decline 791, deleteReq 839); cited line 95-98 slightly off but method names correct.

points.point: Confirms isNotAuthorizedUser(Set) overload exists; HashSet ordering note 'functionally fine'

missed: Approver-only permissions gate the requestor-facing /getTopicRequests, /topic/request/{id}, /getConnectorRequests endpoints — breaks requestor-only roles
/createClaimTopicRequest and /getTopicTeam left without any permission check
Human point: redundant null-check / signature cleanup in PermissionAllowedAspectInterceptor

qualitative: v6 clearly outperforms v5 here. v5's lead 'critical' finding — that the aspect would never run because no AOP dependency exists in any pom — is a verified fabrication: spring-boot-starter-data-jpa transitively supplies spring-boot-starter-aop/aspectjweaver under the Boot 3.2.0 parent, and the PR's own integration tests asserting 401 prove the advice fires; opening with a false blocker badly undermines the review. v5's remaining points (exception base class, annotation-vs-service permission mismatch, residual checks inventory) are accurate and well-verified, but the −1 plus four misses leaves it underwater. v6 produced six verified, accurate points with zero fabrications, including the genuinely subtle ineffective-@Order placement and the missing log.error, and its tool-assisted verification of the isNotAuthorizedUser(Set) overload shows the git tools being used to confirm rather than invent. Both candidates missed the PR's most consequential issue — gating the requestor-facing 'my requests' endpoints behind approver-only permissions — and the unprotected /createClaimTopicRequest, as well as the human reviewer's dead null-check cleanup.
